other_observers: None.; Curt Campbell found the swans earlier that morning.
description: The three adults were entirely white, with long necks. Even at the distance, I could clearly see their black eyes distinctly, connected to their bills by a miniscule amount of black lores. Their bills were smallish with curved culmens. At least one bird had a small, yellow patch, looking like a teardrop coming from one eye. Also, the white cheek feathering against the black bill clearly showed the straight perpendicular contact below the gape, and then an abrupt curve back towards the eye. The two immatures were the same size as the adults, with mostly pinkish bills.Behaviors: Swimming together as a group.Call: none heardPlumage: adult and immature
photographer: Photographer: Doug Faulkner, Doug Faulkner, Doug Faulkner, Doug Faulkner, Doug Faulkner
